prestazioni: Engine swap to a Mitsubishi 4B11T (from the Lancer Ralliart/Evo X) with forged internals (JE 87mm pistons, Eagle H-beam rods, ACL race bearings) and a Garrett GTX3071R turbocharger (0.63 A/R turbine housing) running 22 psi on E85 fuel, paired with a Haltech Elite 2500 ECU for precise boost control and ignition timing

Full bolt-on drivetrain reinforcement: Cusco 1.5-way LSD for the F5MGA transfer case, PTT short-throw shifter with carbon fiber shift knob, and a South Bend Clutch Stage 3 daily/street twin-disc setup with a billet flywheel to handle the 350+ lb-ft torque target

Custom 3-inch turbo-back exhaust system by Tomei (catless downpipe, 76mm mid-pipe, and dual 3.5-inch outlets with Invidia N1-style mufflers), paired with a Mishimoto aluminum radiator and dual 12-inch SPAL fans for optimal cooling under sustained boost
estetica: Liberty Walk LB-WORKS widebody kit (front fenders, rear quarter panels, and rear bumper) with custom carbon fiber overfenders to fit 20x9.5 ET+15 (front) and 20x10.5 ET+20 (rear) Work Emotion CR Kiwami wheels wrapped in 255/30/20 (front) and 285/30/20 (rear) Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 tires

Custom front lip and rear diffuser by Rocket Bunny (Pandem) with integrated LED light bars, paired with a Seibon carbon fiber hood (with functional vents) and a custom rear wing (inspired by the Mitsubishi Evolution X MR Touring, but 3D-scanned to fit the Outlander Sport’s roofline)

Full interior overhaul with Bride Zeta III bucket seats (fixed-back, FIA-approved), Takata 4-point harnesses, a custom roll cage by Custom Cages (bolt-in, 6-point with harness bar), and a full Alcantara-wrapped dashboard with a digital gauge cluster (AEM X-Series 7-inch display) replacing the stock center console
